info.title: Rebecca API
info.description:
Go-native Rebecca panel API. This document replaces the former FastAPI /docs page and is intended for dashboard, bot, and third-party client developers.

Authentication: protected routes use `Authorization: Bearer <token>`. The token can be the JWT returned by `POST /api/admin/token`; admin API keys are also accepted by the Go auth layer where bearer validation is used. Public subscription and home/docs routes explicitly set `security: []`.

Response compatibility: most endpoints intentionally keep the JSON shape expected by the current dashboard. Some schemas are documented as generic JSON while the migration to strict OpenAPI component schemas continues.

Mutation behavior: user, service, node, config, and host mutations are designed to be transactional. When runtime sync is required, Rebecca writes `node_operations` in the same transaction so failed queue writes roll back the database change.

tags.name: Docs
tags.description: Interactive API documentation and OpenAPI schema. Swagger UI is served offline from the Go binary; no CDN is required.

tags.name: Admin
tags.description: Admin authentication, management, permissions, API keys, and usage analytics. Use these routes to build admin management tools. Standard admins are scope-limited; sudo/full_access admins can manage global state. Login report delivery is currently disabled while Telegram is rebuilt in Go.

tags.name: My Account
tags.description: Current admin account settings and personal API keys. These routes always operate on the authenticated admin. Password changes update password_reset_at and invalidate older JWTs.

tags.name: Users
tags.description: User read, mutation, lifecycle, usage, and subscription operations. User creation now requires a service assignment. Manual inbound selection is removed; use service_id or the legacy setservice-<id> auto-service inbound only. Legacy proxies payloads are accepted for compatibility, and vmess/vless IDs are normalized into credential_key without persisting new proxy rows. Mutations enqueue node operations when runtime user state must change.

tags.name: Services
tags.description: Service management, hosts, service limits, service-scoped users, and usage analytics. Service links drive generated subscription configs. Changing service hosts does not change subscription URLs, but it changes config output and queues runtime sync where needed.

tags.name: Nodes
tags.description: Node management, certificates, runtime control, logs, metrics, and usage. Node control uses Go gRPC/mTLS. Node-down states should not break queued user mutations; operational calls return direct node errors.

tags.name: Core
tags.description: Core runtime, config targets, geo templates, helper utilities, WARP, and outbound tooling. Master is node-only and does not run local Xray. Config writes save DB state and synchronize nodes rather than starting a local core.

tags.name: Inbounds
tags.description: Xray inbound registry and manageable inbound configuration. Inbounds are configuration registry objects. User-level manual inbound selection has been removed; service hosts are the active routing model.

tags.name: Hosts
tags.description: Subscription host management grouped by inbound tag. Hosts are the public subscription endpoints attached to services through service_hosts ordering.

tags.name: Settings
tags.description: Panel settings, subscription settings, templates, backup import/export, and disabled migration TODO routes. Backup routes are binary-install only. Certificate and 3x-ui importer routes are documented but intentionally return 410 until rebuilt.

tags.name: System
tags.description: System statistics and maintenance actions. Maintenance actions are restricted and are intended for binary installations only.

tags.name: Subscriptions
tags.description: User subscription links, info, usage, aliases, and client-specific config formats. Subscription links are public and resolve users by token, credential key, username/key, aliases, and client-type suffixes. Output format can also depend on User-Agent.

tags.name: Home
tags.description: Public home page template. The home page is template-backed and public.

post.description:
Authenticate an admin and issue a JWT access token. This is the compatibility alias used by the former FastAPI OAuth2 form route.

Authentication: public route; no bearer token is required.

Permissions: Admin routes require an authenticated bearer token except token creation and internal validation. Mutations generally require admin-management permission or sudo/full_access; full_access role changes require a full_access actor.

Request body: accepts `application/x-www-form-urlencoded` for OAuth2-compatible clients and JSON for programmatic clients.

Side effects: this is a mutation. Database writes are transactional where applicable; runtime-affecting changes enqueue node operations or call the node controller as documented by the route.

get.description:
Return Telegram integration settings from the database, including bot token, proxy URL, admin/log/backup chat destinations, forum topic IDs, notification event toggles, and periodic backup schedule. Bot command handling is still intentionally disabled; this route only manages delivery settings used by Go-native reports and backups.

Authentication: send `Authorization: Bearer <token>` using a JWT from `POST /api/admin/token` or a valid admin API key.

Permissions: Telegram settings require sudo/full_access because the response can include the bot token and chat destinations.

put.description:
Update Telegram delivery settings. `admin_chat_ids` is normalized to a de-duplicated integer list. `logs_chat_id` controls report/notification delivery, while `backup_chat_id` can override the backup destination. `forum_topics` stores per-category Telegram topic/thread IDs, and `event_toggles` stores report switches such as user/admin/node/login events. `proxy_url` supports http, https, socks5, and socks5h.

Authentication: send `Authorization: Bearer <token>` using a JWT from `POST /api/admin/token` or a valid admin API key.

Permissions: Telegram settings require sudo/full_access because the request can mutate bot credentials and delivery targets.

Request body: JSON object. Unknown fields are ignored for compatibility; invalid proxy URLs, chat IDs, backup scope, or interval units return 400.

post.description:
Create a Rebecca backup with the Go backup service and send it to Telegram immediately. The route reads the stored Telegram bot token, proxy URL, backup chat destination, admin-chat fallback, and backup topic/thread settings from `telegram_settings`. The request may specify `scope` as `database` or `full`; if omitted, the stored periodic backup scope is used. Large backup archives are split into multiple Telegram documents and each part gets a part-number caption. Delivery status is written back to `backup_last_sent_at` or `backup_last_error`.

Authentication: send `Authorization: Bearer <token>` using a JWT from `POST /api/admin/token` or a valid admin API key.

Permissions: sudo/full_access is required because this operation exports database/files and sends them to an external Telegram chat.

Runtime constraints: backup export is available only in binary installation mode. Docker/non-binary installations return 409. Telegram delivery errors return 400/502 depending on whether the failure is configuration-related or remote-delivery-related.

Send a Telegram test message using the stored bot token, proxy, and destination settings. If a `chat_id` is not provided, Rebecca uses the log chat first and then the first admin chat. The route records the last Telegram error in `telegram_settings.last_error`, which is also surfaced by `/api/system` as `last_telegram_error`.

Authentication: send `Authorization: Bearer <token>` using a JWT from `POST /api/admin/token` or a valid admin API key.

Permissions: sudo/full_access is required because this sends an external message and validates secret bot credentials.

Request body: optional JSON object with `chat_id` and `text`.

get.summary: Get latest panel maintenance operation status
get.description: Returns the latest host-level update, restart, or soft-reload operation tracked by the Go API. Dashboard clients poll this endpoint while the maintenance dialog is open to show command output, parsed download percentage, current phase, and whether the panel is entering restart. The state is intentionally in-memory and resets after the Rebecca process comes back.

bearerAuth.bearerFormat: JWT or Rebecca admin API key

Username.name: username
Username.description: Rebecca username. Matching is case-insensitive in most user lookup paths.

AdminIDQuery.name: admin_id
AdminIDQuery.description: Admin ID to chart. Some service analytics allow `null` for unassigned users.

ServiceID.name: service_id
ServiceID.description: Numeric service ID from `/api/v2/services`. Service 0/null is reserved for legacy/internal behavior and should not be used for new users.

Offset.name: offset
Offset.description: Zero-based row offset used for paginated list endpoints.

Limit.name: limit
Limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return. Large values may be capped by server-side settings.

Start.name: start
Start.description: Start of the analytics range. ISO 8601 is preferred; server falls back to its default range when omitted.

End.name: end
End.description: End of the analytics range. ISO 8601 is preferred; server falls back to now when omitted.

Granularity.name: granularity
Granularity.description: Analytics bucket size. Use `hour` for detailed charts and `day` for longer ranges.

JSON.description: Generic JSON request body. See each operation description for required business fields and side effects.

LoginForm.description: Admin login credentials. Form encoding is compatible with OAuth2PasswordRequestForm; JSON is accepted by the Go implementation for API clients.

JSON.description: JSON response. Exact object fields follow the dashboard-compatible Rebecca API response for the route.

Error.description: Error response. Most handlers return `{ "detail": "..." }`; some compatibility routes may return a plain text body when handled by net/http helpers.
